title: pilosella floribunda subsp. hylaeophila
intro: Pilosella floribunda subsp. hylaeophila, also known as the alpine hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the sunflower family, native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been found to exhibit medicinal properties such as wound healing, pain relief, and immune system support. The plant's extracts have been shown to have various health benefits, including antioxidant, antimicrobial, and anti-cancer properties, making it a promising herbal remedy. Herbal preparations of Pilosella floribunda subsp. hylaeophila typically involve infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential uses and effects.
